subject: Gaming
title: Experience Points and Progress Bars
description: A quick, practical guide to Experience Points and Progress Bars.

hook.text: Watching an XP bar fill up gives your brain a dopamine hit. Progress feels good even when it's meaningless.

buildup.text: RPGs invented experience points. Now every genre—shooters, racers, even fitness apps—uses XP.

discovery.text: The progress bar creates a 'goal gradient effect.' You push harder as you approach completion.

twist.text: Many games give massive XP early and reduce it later. The feeling of fast progress hooks you before the grind.

climax.text: XP bars work because they make invisible progress visible. Seeing growth is inherently satisfying.

punchline.text: Watch the bar fill. Feel the dopamine. Progress is its own reward.

quiz.question: What is the goal gradient effect?
options: People work harder as they get closer to completing a goal
Goals become less interesting over time
